title: Seeles Plan for Instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: Seele's Plan for Instrumentality             Quote Post by someguy » Thu Sep 08, 2011 6:31 am I tried doing some research and couldn't find some of this, so hopefully these aren't all old points I'm not familiar with. I was never satisfied with the amount of information presented about Seele's true plans for Instrumentality. While we are given the impression their plans vary from those of Gendo, we aren't told exactly what they want, but the series very repeatedly drives home the point that things aren't going according to Seele's plans and the problem is growing considerably in the last few episodes. Then (in EoE), Rei defies Gendo and the Shinji-centric Instrumentality happens and apparently this is all fine and dandy for Seele, with Keel happily popping into LCL without even needing a transition guide. This never sat well with me, that Seele is presented as the dark brotherhood secretly steering society towards their own goals but their apparent goal of merging humanity into a ball of happy doesn't seem very evil (obviously that point is debatable but I don't want that to be the debate here). But then I looked at the Classified Information translation here, and it has some very interesting points: "Seele believed they had through Eva what was necessary to fill-in that which was missing; to make man into a God, or at least into an eternal existence. Completing an incomplete humanity, and opening up the Path to God, is Seele's doctrine. What would happen if people were gathered into something of a god? Seele believed we would become God himself. The first step is the completion of Eva — the body of a god and throne of a soul — via the installation of an S² Engine. The interfusion of souls follows. Afterward, our final natural enemy, the Spear of Longinus security device, is annihilated. Thus, that which is nearly divine, or perhaps a god in and of itself, is brought to completion, and, with the Spear gone, cannot be destroyed by anyone. Seele's intention for this man-made god is to guide the elite (themselves) to state near that of God's." So does this mean Seele's goal was to merge all of humanity into an Eva to make it a God containing the sum of all humanity inside it? This is very different than simply creating the ball of happy. When I see descriptions and analysis of Seele's plan for 3I and instrumentality I don't recall seeing people talk about Seele merging everyone into an Eva (or maybe I just missed something). There is dialog from Seele about 5 minutes into EoE which might support this... a committee member says "says we need not cast aside our human form to use Evangelion as our own private Ark" (I also found this translated as "We are planning to get a ride on the Ark called EVA, but it will not change our human shape") I know, this contrasts with the oft-cited Seele statement that they don't wish to create a God, but I wonder if this line has been interpreted correctly. Watching that scene, I think they could be saying (though this depends on translation accuracy) that they have no use for the actualized version of a God that Gendo created, with a defiant soul already guiding its actions, before they were ready. Also, it might be folly to take everything Seele says at face value. If they wished to use EVA as an ark without changing human shape, this is incredibly contrary to the usually presented theory they simply wanted everyone to pop and merge into the happy ball... but then why are they so happy with that ending? From the dialog in the film and the Classified Information it seems clear the Seele members intended to create an Eva-based God, and in some way retain human form. Do only the "elite" mentioned in the CI maintain human form? And how exactly is this defined? Do they want to maintain their current bodies, or do they consider the Evas, as a God created in mankind's image, to be "human form"? I at first thought it was very relevant that there are (normally) 12 committee members, and with the nine production Evas a total of 12 Evas, but from their reaction to Eva-01 ingesting the S2 I get the impression Seele did not plan to put S2 engines in the original Eva units (and if you count Eva-03 and 04 there would have been fourteen units, which is more than 12 but not enough to cover Episode 24's 15 Seele members). Anyway, assuming the CI information is accurate, it seems there is more to Seele's plan than can be inferred from the series by itself.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Sep 08, 2011 10:59 am Apparently, if you actually play the Eva 2 game which gave us the C.I. through to the "Seele Wins" ending, their plan is that only Seele enters Eva and becomes a god; everyone else is screwed: [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=8742[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Sep 08, 2011 11:57 pm But that aside, the general consensus of the idea was that all souls merged into one entity that felt no sorrow or happiness and had no emptiness, and thus no motivations. It'd be "God" in a more Taoist sense since it'd be so tranquil that it would probably never do anything. It's basically an extremely egotistical form of committing suicide.

[]:             Quote Post by Razeil100 » Fri Sep 09, 2011 12:52 am There has a been a huge influx of new posters in the past month. Was someone advertising? Anyways, yeah SEELE wanted all of humanity to transcend emotion and corporeal obstacles to happiness, basically do away with the pain of life, although all you'd really get with tangification is supreme apathy. Which is odd, because all Shinji seems to do is scream and choke Asuka during it.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Sep 09, 2011 2:39 am The problem is that in the process of everyone merging into one, everyone is going to exposed to everyone else's thoughts. Which is going to feel way worse than physical rape ever could and it's going to be billions of times over. So it's basically five minutes of hell for an eternity of "peace".

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Sep 09, 2011 3:26 am AuraTwilight wrote: But that aside, the general consensus of the idea was that all souls merged into one entity that felt no sorrow or happiness and had no emptiness, and thus no motivations.

[]:             Quote Post by someguy » Fri Sep 09, 2011 5:06 am There has a been a huge influx of new posters in the past month. Was someone advertising?

[]:             Quote Post by Razeil100 » Fri Sep 09, 2011 5:11 am someguy wrote: I just rewatched the series for the first time since 2006, and there's a lot more information and analysis available now then there was then. Sorry for being new and lacking social skills

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Fri Sep 09, 2011 1:06 pm thewayneiac wrote: Apparently, if you actually play the Eva 2 game which gave us the C.I. through to the "Seele Wins" ending, their plan is that only Seele enters Eva and becomes a god; everyone else is screwed: [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=8742[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by someguy » Fri Sep 09, 2011 6:56 pm I added the information from the game to the 3I, Seele, and HIP articles (greatly expanding the latter two in the process).
